threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube.' Locals here just hop on the 'Overground' like it's the most natural thing in the world.
Last night on the 59, a woman serenaded a guy with a ukulele—wasn’t half bad. Only in Brixton would that be a normal Saturday.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid on the playground teaching his dog to skate. That mix of carefree and chaotic is Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand open at 3 AM, serving up spicy sausages to a crowd sharing tales of wild nights and lost phones.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid the BART Civic Center for the next week, not just for the train, but the chaos it brings.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 last week; driver's casual chat about 'another dog on the tracks' had us all smirking, but man, it wasn't just a joke anymore.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals speak perfect English. But in Gràcia, you’ll find a lot of folks sticking to Catalan, especially in the smaller bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their confused looks while trying to navigate the confusing one-way streets; the locals just glide by without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Tube' instead of just 'the Overground'—people here are big on their train lines.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded us with 'Wonderwall' using a peanut butter jar as a mic. Brixton's late-night vibes never disappoint.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I caught sight of the elderly lady at the bakery, sharing fresh bread with the street cats every morning. That’s when I truly felt at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop where the owner doubles as a DJ, spinning tunes while serving up döner at 3 AM. It’s a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively duck when walking past the BART entrance on a Friday evening. Always a good reason to be cautious.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Every time I pass Civic Center, I can’t help but wonder if the dog on the tracks is part of some urban art project gone rogue.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all about fancy boutiques, but the real charm is in the tiny, bustling corners where locals still gather for a simple café amb llet.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newbie by their bewildered look staring at the bus schedule. They still expect everything to run on time.
